raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-affirmation of autonomy and internal epistemic authority, functioning as a cognitive rehearsal to reduce reliance on external validation",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "an internal compass exists and is reliably accessible",
    "truth can be maintained independently of interpersonal agreement",
    "clarity is attainable as a stable internal state rather than a negotiated or provisional one",
    "prior dependence on others' agreement has been or can be relinquished without loss of accuracy"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent; each clause reinforces the others by moving from self-ownership of cognition to epistemic independence to affective stabilization, with no logical contradictions",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the speaker's internal signals are sufficiently reality-oriented, the statement is used to counter excessive external dependence rather than to dismiss all feedback, and it is practiced within a therapeutic context that can reality-test the compass when needed",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the internal compass is systematically biased or distorted, the statement is deployed to justify avoidance of corrective information, or it reinforces a false dichotomy between autonomy and accurate social feedback"
}
